behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler orchestration across signature verification and queue handoff (`401` plus `not forwarded`), which is a module interaction (MOCK-MISS). No behavior text requires real network/OS/subprocess (REAL-INFRA no), no AI output (LLM-DEP no), and no multi-agent chain (MULTI-STEP no). Rejected System because the behavior text is about response/forwarding logic, not real HTTP transport faults.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler with real verifier + queue adapter wiring and a malformed `X-Signature-256`; assert status `401` and assert queue `enqueue` was not called/persisted.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee between queue persistence and response emission across modules; unit-testing one function is too low (MOCK-MISS yes). No explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess beyond local module wiring, and no model dependency. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real transport conditions, only write-before-ack ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Use app+queue modules together with a controllable queue write (e.g., promise barrier). Verify `200` is not sent until write resolves, and is sent immediately after successful write.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in handoff semantics between handler, queue, and downstream worker lifecycle; needs module interaction. No real external infra is required by text; a blocking downstream stub can expose waiting bugs. No AI/multi-step agentics. Rejected System because behavior is about non-blocking control flow, not OS/network-specific failure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to a downstream processor stub that blocks for a long duration; assert response is returned once enqueue succeeds and before processor completion.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrency, which is sensitive to real runtime scheduling and real HTTP request handling (REAL-INFRA yes, MOCK-HIDE likely). Integration fakes can miss event-loop starvation/backpressure timing effects. No AI dependency and no agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service process with real HTTP listener; generate concurrent traffic while earlier events trigger intentionally slow downstream work; measure end-to-end response time and assert each new webhook response is <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at boundary between handler and persistent queue/idempotency store across requests, not a single pure function (MOCK-MISS yes). No requirement for real external OS/network/subprocess; local persistence wiring is enough. No AI/multi-agent dependency. Rejected System because text specifies duplicate-write prevention logic, not infrastructure-specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Send two requests with same event ID through wired handler+queue persistence; assert first is accepted/written and second does not create another queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Failure is translation of queue-full condition into HTTP semantics across module boundary (queue -> handler). Can be caught with deterministic queue-full signal; no real OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text. No AI/multi-step dependency. Rejected System because this does not require validating real network failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Inject/wire queue implementation that returns `full`; invoke handler and assert status `503` plus correct `Retry-After` header and no enqueue side effects.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets real queue component behavior with its persistence layer/config interaction; a pure unit fake could hide true capacity enforcement defects (MOCK-HIDE yes). Still local DB/filesystem-level wiring fits Integration per level defs. Rejected System because behavior text does not require external network/OS primitives beyond local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against local test storage with capacity `N`; enqueue `N` events successfully, then assert enqueue `N+1` is rejected with the queue’s overflow error/signal.
